insights.insight: Verified receipt signals are settling at ~$0.10 per confirmed conversion event versus $5–$150 CPA for probabilistic ad tech attribution — a 50–200x cost gap that reflects the structural difference between cryptographic proof and statistical inference.

insights.topic: Cookie deprecation leaves a real gap
insights.insight: With Chrome joining Safari and Firefox in blocking cross-site cookies, device fingerprinting under regulatory pressure, and data brokers shrinking under GDPR/CCPA, no current cookieless alternative produces identity-free, cross-ecosystem, verified purchase signals — this protocol is the only candidate in that category with live receipts.

insights.topic: Cross-ecosystem blind spot is the silo problem
insights.insight: Amazon, Walmart, and Kroger each see only their own purchase data; no platform sees across the ecosystem, and none will share customer records with competitors — verified receipt tokens carry the commerce fact without the identity, making cross-retailer signal possible for the first time without data-sharing agreements.

Nine non-metro state designations are active across physical retail receipts from Walmart, Kroger, Dollar General, and Home Depot — the geographic spread is following social graphs, not ad spend, which means the dataset is capturing a consumer segment that metro-first digital targeting routinely underweights.

insights.topic: Three distinct purchase cohorts on one infrastructure
insights.insight: Physical retail consumers, AI/SaaS subscribers, and developer infrastructure buyers are all submitting receipts through the same verification layer simultaneously — for brands, this means a single query surface across purchase categories that would otherwise require three separate data partnerships.

insights.topic: Dataset compounding as the real moat
insights.insight: The competitive advantage here is not the cryptographic standards, which are open — it is the accumulating receipt dataset itself; at 3,893 verified receipts and $227K GMV, the pool is early, but the dynamic is self-reinforcing: query value rises with scale, which draws more receipts, which raises query value.
